project.name: MeganeCAN
project.description: ESP32-based CAN-bus companion firmware for Renault Megane 2 infotainment displays. Acts as a smart sidecar on the car CAN bus: emulates the head-unit radio to render menus and text on the OEM display, bridges phone media controls over BLE (Apple Media Service or BLE Keyboard), exposes a PsychicHttp web UI with OTA updates, and offers optional ELM327/OBD diagnostics over WiFi. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: IsoTp.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements the AFFA3 ISO-TP framing helpers: fragments a payload into a first-frame plus consecutive CAN frames with protocol filler, and reassembles inbound multi-frame sequences.

nodes.name: IsoTp.h
nodes.summary: Declares the ISO-TP fragment helper and a Reassembler class that accumulates consecutive CAN frames into a complete AFFA3 payload buffer.
nodes.languageNotes: Header-only Reassembler with fixed char buffer to avoid heap churn on the RAM-tight ESP32.

nodes.name: ScreenDecode.cpp
nodes.summary: Decodes reassembled AFFA3 payloads into a ScreenModel: extracts ASCII-trimmed text fields, menu header/items, and 8-segment text for the virtual display decoders.

nodes.name: ScreenDecode.h
nodes.summary: Declares the ScreenDecode namespace API (asciiz, menu, segText, frame) used to turn raw AFFA3 frames into a structured ScreenModel.

nodes.name: ScreenModel.h
nodes.summary: Defines the ScreenModel data structure representing decoded display state (mode, header, menu items, selection, scroll, info lines) shared across virtual display decoders.

nodes.name: Frame.h
nodes.summary: Defines the transport-agnostic CAN Frame struct (id, len, data, extended) used throughout the bus abstraction and decode layers.

nodes.name: CarminatConstants.h
nodes.summary: Holds the Carminat protocol CAN IDs and constants (sync, control, text, key identifiers) used by the Carminat display driver and its virtual display.

nodes.name: CarminatVirtualDisplay.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements the Carminat virtual display decode: reassembles ISO-TP frames on the control ID and renders the decoded menu/now-playing screen via ScreenDecode.

nodes.name: CarminatVirtualDisplay.h
nodes.summary: Declares the CarminatVirtualDisplay class, a PC-side virtual Carminat display that extends VirtualDisplayBase with the Carminat protocol descriptor.

nodes.name: IVirtualDisplay.h
nodes.summary: Pure interface for PC-side virtual displays exposing begin, onCanRx, and pressKey, decoupling the emulator from concrete protocol implementations.

nodes.name: UpdateListLcdVirtualDisplay.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements the UpdateList monochrome-LCD virtual display decode, reassembling ISO-TP frames and rendering the decoded menu screen via ScreenDecode.

nodes.name: UpdateListLcdVirtualDisplay.h
nodes.summary: Declares the UpdateListLcdVirtualDisplay class, the LCD-mode UpdateList virtual display that reuses the UpdateList protocol descriptor from the segment variant.

nodes.name: UpdateListSegVirtualDisplay.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements the UpdateList 8-segment virtual display decode, reassembling text frames and rendering segment text via ScreenDecode::segText.

nodes.name: UpdateListSegVirtualDisplay.h
nodes.summary: Declares the UpdateListSegVirtualDisplay class and the shared UpdateList VdProtocol descriptor (CAN IDs and filler) reused by the LCD variant.

nodes.name: VirtualDisplayBase.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements the shared virtual-display engine: ISO-TP reassembly dispatch on inbound CAN frames, registration auto-ACK/sync handshake, and key-press encoding back onto the bus.

nodes.name: VirtualDisplayBase.h
nodes.summary: Declares VirtualDisplayBase (common emulator state machine over ICanBus/IClock) and the VdProtocol struct describing per-protocol CAN IDs, reply flags, and filler bytes.

nodes.name: test_vdisplay.cpp
nodes.summary: Unity test suite for the virtual display stack: ISO-TP round-trip, ASCII trimming, Carminat menu/highlight/key/sync decode, and UpdateList segment and LCD key handling over a loopback CAN bus and fake clock.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the Unity C test framework with LoopbackCanBus and FakeClock seams to exercise decoders host-side without hardware.

nodes.name: fragment
nodes.summary: Splits a payload into AFFA3 ISO-TP frames: a 7-byte first frame followed by [0x20+N]-indexed consecutive frames, padding the tail with the protocol filler byte.

nodes.name: onFrame
nodes.summary: Reassembler frame handler that detects a first frame, then appends consecutive-frame payload bytes until the full ISO-TP message is reconstructed.

nodes.name: Reassembler
nodes.summary: Stateful ISO-TP reassembly buffer accumulating consecutive CAN frames into a complete payload, exposing len/active/reset accessors over a fixed internal buffer.

nodes.name: asciiz
nodes.summary: Extracts a byte range from an AFFA3 payload into a null-terminated, whitespace-trimmed ASCII string with bounds checking against the destination size.

nodes.name: menu
nodes.summary: Decodes a menu/now-playing payload into the ScreenModel header and two item fields by extracting their fixed byte ranges via asciiz.

nodes.name: segText
nodes.summary: Decodes the UpdateList 8-segment text payload into the ScreenModel's text fields using asciiz extraction.

nodes.name: frame
nodes.summary: Inspects a raw CAN frame to classify the AFFA3 message type (e.g. sync/registration) and updates the ScreenModel mode accordingly.

nodes.name: ScreenModel
nodes.summary: Decoded display state holder (mode, header, two menu items with IDs, selection, scroll lock, and info lines) populated by ScreenDecode and consumed by virtual displays.

nodes.name: Frame
nodes.summary: Plain CAN frame value type carrying id, length, up to 8 data bytes, and an extended-id flag, used as the common currency of the bus and decode layers.

nodes.name: decode
nodes.summary: Carminat virtual display frame decoder: classifies the frame, feeds control-ID frames into the ISO-TP reassembler, and decodes the completed payload into the screen menu model.

nodes.name: CarminatVirtualDisplay
nodes.summary: PC-side virtual Carminat display subclass of VirtualDisplayBase, configured with the Carminat protocol descriptor and overriding decode for menu rendering.

nodes.name: IVirtualDisplay
nodes.summary: Abstract base interface for virtual displays declaring begin, onCanRx, and pressKey so the emulator can drive any protocol variant uniformly.

nodes.name: decode
nodes.summary: UpdateList LCD virtual display frame decoder: reassembles ISO-TP frames and decodes the completed payload into the menu screen model.

nodes.name: UpdateListLcdVirtualDisplay
nodes.summary: UpdateList monochrome-LCD virtual display subclass reusing the UpdateList protocol descriptor and overriding decode for menu rendering.

nodes.name: decode
nodes.summary: UpdateList 8-segment virtual display frame decoder: reassembles text frames and decodes them into segment text via ScreenDecode::segText.

nodes.name: UpdateListSegVirtualDisplay
nodes.summary: UpdateList 8-segment virtual display subclass that owns the shared UpdateList VdProtocol descriptor and overrides decode for segment text rendering.

nodes.name: autoAck
nodes.summary: Emits the registration/per-frame ACK reply on the protocol's reply ID so the firmware's affa3_do_send completes its multi-frame send against the virtual display.

nodes.name: onCanRx
nodes.summary: Inbound CAN handler that routes frames into the protocol decoder and, when emulating, drives the sync handshake and sends auto-ACK replies.

nodes.name: pressKey
nodes.summary: Encodes a key-press (with optional hold) into the protocol's key CAN frame and sends it on the bus, emulating a physical display button.

nodes.name: VirtualDisplayBase
nodes.summary: Shared base for all virtual displays implementing IVirtualDisplay over an ICanBus/IClock, holding ISO-TP reassembly, screen state, sync, and emulation/ack-mode flags.

nodes.name: VdProtocol
nodes.summary: Protocol descriptor struct binding per-protocol CAN IDs (ctrl, text, key, sync, sync reply), reply flag, and filler byte that parameterize the virtual display base.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Unity test runner registering and executing all virtual-display decode, key codec, and sync test cases.

nodes.name: test_isotp_roundtrip
nodes.summary: Verifies that IsoTp::fragment output fed back through the Reassembler reconstructs the original menu payload byte-for-byte.

nodes.name: test_carminat_menu_decode
nodes.summary: Drives a fragmented Carminat menu frame through the virtual display and asserts the decoded header, items, selection, and auto-ACK reply on the loopback bus.

nodes.name: test_carminat_key_codec
nodes.summary: Exercises pressKey on the Carminat virtual display and asserts the emitted key CAN frame ID and byte encoding for press and hold.

nodes.name: test_ul_seg_decode
nodes.summary: Feeds a fragmented UpdateList segment-text frame through the virtual display and asserts the decoded text fields and auto-ACK reply.

nodes.name: apple_notification_service.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements the Apple Notification Center Service (ANCS) GATT client: subscribes to the notification source/data source characteristics, parses incoming iPhone notifications, requests their attributes, and exposes recent notifications. Callbacks defer work and never block the BLE host.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a FreeRTOS mutex RAII guard (Lock) to serialize access to the notification queue from BLE callback context.

nodes.name: apple_notification_service.h
nodes.summary: Header for the ANCS client declaring the NotificationInfo model (category, uid, app id, title, message) and the service's public API for registration, retrieval, and processing.

nodes.name: bluetooth.cpp
nodes.summary: BLE peripheral orchestrator: advertises the device (name plus AMS solicitation in the primary packet), accepts the iPhone's inbound connection, relaxes connection params, and on a secured link starts the AMS, ANCS, and CTS GATT clients. Drives bonding, status JSON, and the per-loop Service() state machine.
nodes.languageNotes: Advertising puts the name and AMS solicitation in the primary packet (<=31 bytes); connection callbacks defer GATT work to avoid deadlocking the NimBLE host.

nodes.name: bluetooth.h
nodes.summary: Header declaring the Bluetooth namespace API: Begin, Service, connection/bond queries, ClearBonds, and status JSON used by the main loop, serial console, and HTTP server.

nodes.name: current_time_service.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements the Current Time Service (CTS) GATT client: subscribes to and reads the current-time characteristic from the iPhone, parses it into a CurrentTime struct, and converts to time_t for the system clock.

nodes.name: current_time_service.h
nodes.summary: Header for the CTS client declaring the CurrentTime struct (year/month/day/time/day-of-week) with Dump and ToTimeT helpers and the StartTimeService API.

nodes.name: Affa3TestPage.h
nodes.summary: Embedded HTML page (PROGMEM string) for the AFFA3 display test/debug UI served by the HTTP server.

nodes.name: DashboardPage.h
nodes.summary: Embedded HTML/JS dashboard page (PROGMEM string) that polls the single /api/dashboard endpoint and renders media, notifications, Bluetooth, WiFi, and CAN status.

nodes.name: DiagPage.h
nodes.summary: Embedded HTML page (PROGMEM string) for the ELM327/OBD diagnostics UI rendering the diagnostic snapshot JSON.

nodes.name: HttpServerManager.cpp
nodes.summary: PsychicHttp server manager: registers all configuration, media/notification, OTA, WiFi, diagnostics, and emulation routes, and builds streaming JSON for the unified dashboard endpoint. Bridges HTTP requests to display, Bluetooth, WiFi, and ELM subsystems.
nodes.languageNotes: Routes prefer fixed char[] / streaming over String churn and config setters call ESP.restart() to reload the cached AppConfig (RAM-tight device).

nodes.name: WirePage.h
nodes.summary: Embedded HTML/JS page (PROGMEM string) for the wire/serial-proxy view that streams @TX/@RX/@EV frames over the WebSocket WsWireLink.

nodes.name: AppConfig.cpp
nodes.summary: Loads firmware configuration from NVS into a RAM cache at boot (display_type, bt_mode, auto_time, elm_enabled, skip_funcreg) and provides typed getters plus provisioning helpers, avoiding per-request NVS churn.

nodes.name: AppConfig.h
nodes.summary: Header declaring the AppConfig RAM-cached configuration API: Load, displayKind/btKind enums, and provisioning state accessors used across the firmware.

nodes.name: Log.cpp
nodes.summary: Minimal logging utility implementing a timestamped printf wrapper over the serial port (the RAM ring log was removed in favor of the serial proxy).

nodes.name: Log.h
nodes.summary: Header declaring the Log namespace's printf/dump/clear logging API used throughout the firmware.

nodes.name: wifi_manager.cpp
nodes.summary: WiFiManager implementation: connects in STA mode using NVS-persisted home credentials with AP fallback, starts mDNS (meganecan.local) in STA, and performs heap-gated network scanning exposed as JSON for the web UI.
nodes.languageNotes: WiFi scans are gated on the largest contiguous heap block (getMaxAllocHeap) because the device is RAM-tight with BLE+HTTP live.

nodes.name: wifi_manager.h
nodes.summary: Header declaring the WiFiManager namespace API: Begin, Handle, scanning, credential persistence, and status accessors (mode, SSID, IP, hostname).

nodes.name: NotificationInfo
nodes.summary: Data model for a single ANCS notification holding category id, uid, originating app id, title, and message text.

nodes.name: onNotificationSource
nodes.summary: ANCS notification-source callback: handles added/modified/removed notification events, maintains the notification list under lock, and queues attribute requests.

nodes.name: onDataSource
nodes.summary: ANCS data-source callback: parses the attribute response (title/message/app id) for a uid and dispatches the completed notification to the registered callback.

nodes.name: requestAttributes
nodes.summary: Writes a GetNotificationAttributes command to the ANCS control point to request title, message, and app identifier for a notification uid.

nodes.name: Process
nodes.summary: Per-loop pump that pops queued notification uids and issues attribute requests, keeping GATT writes off the BLE callback path.

nodes.name: AppName
nodes.summary: Maps an iOS bundle identifier to a human-readable app name, falling back to the trailing component of the bundle id.

nodes.name: StartNotificationService
nodes.summary: Discovers the ANCS service and its notification-source/data-source/control-point characteristics on the connected client and subscribes to notifications.

nodes.name: startCTS
nodes.summary: Starts the Current Time Service client, reads the iPhone clock, and applies it to the system time via settimeofday.

nodes.name: startAdvertising
nodes.summary: Builds and starts BLE advertising with the device name and AMS solicitation packed into the primary advertisement packet so a fresh iPhone surfaces it.

nodes.name: Begin
nodes.summary: Initializes NimBLE as a peripheral, configures MTU and security/bonding, creates the server with callbacks, and starts advertising.

nodes.name: Service
nodes.summary: Per-loop BLE state machine that re-advertises when idle, secures the connection, and once secured starts the AMS, ANCS, and CTS GATT clients and pumps notification processing.

nodes.name: ClearBonds
nodes.summary: Deletes all stored BLE bonds and disconnects the active client, used to recover from corrupt pairing state.

nodes.name: GetStatusJson
nodes.summary: Builds a small JSON object reporting BLE connection and bond status for the dashboard.

nodes.name: ServerCallbacks
nodes.summary: NimBLEServer callback handler reacting to connect/disconnect/authentication events; relaxes connection params on connect and tracks bond state, deferring GATT work out of the callback.

nodes.name: CurrentTime
nodes.summary: Struct modeling the CTS current-time characteristic fields (year, month, day, hours, minutes, seconds, day-of-week, fraction) with Dump and ToTimeT conversion helpers.

nodes.name: ParseCurrentTime
nodes.summary: Parses the raw CTS characteristic byte buffer into a CurrentTime struct.

nodes.name: ToTimeT
nodes.summary: Converts a parsed CurrentTime struct into a Unix time_t via mktime for use with settimeofday.

nodes.name: GetCurrentTime
nodes.summary: Reads the CTS current-time characteristic value and parses it into the provided CurrentTime struct.

nodes.name: StartTimeService
nodes.summary: Discovers the CTS service and current-time characteristic on the connected client, subscribes to updates, and performs an initial read.

nodes.name: setupRoutes
nodes.summary: Registers every HTTP route: text/scroll/welcome display commands, time/voltage, config (display_type, bt_mode, skip_funcreg), bond clearing, WiFi scan/save, diagnostics snapshot, and emulation endpoints.

nodes.name: buildMediaJson
nodes.summary: Builds the now-playing media JSON (title/artist/album/state and progress) from AppleMediaService for the dashboard.

nodes.name: buildNotifsJson
nodes.summary: Builds the recent-notifications JSON array from AppleNotificationService for the dashboard.

nodes.name: begin
nodes.summary: Starts the PsychicHttp server, registers routes and the WebSocket wire link, and attaches OTA.

nodes.name: jsonEsc
nodes.summary: Escapes a string for safe embedding in JSON output.

nodes.name: Load
nodes.summary: Reads all config keys from the NVS 'config' namespace into the RAM cache at boot, applying defaults and one-time migrations.

nodes.name: markProvisioned
nodes.summary: Persists a provisioning flag to NVS marking the device as configured.

nodes.name: printf
nodes.summary: Formats a variadic log message and emits it to serial with a millis() timestamp prefix.

nodes.name: tryStation
nodes.summary: Attempts an STA connection with the given credentials and optional static IP, disabling modem sleep, setting hostname, waiting for the link, and starting mDNS on success.

nodes.name: Begin
nodes.summary: Loads home WiFi credentials from NVS and connects in STA mode, falling back to AP mode when none are stored or the connection fails.

nodes.name: ScanJson
nodes.summary: Runs (or reads) a heap-gated WiFi scan, sorts results by RSSI, caps the count, and serializes them to JSON for the web UI.

nodes.name: scanHeapOk
nodes.summary: Guards WiFi scanning by checking the largest contiguous heap block to avoid OOM on the RAM-tight device.

nodes.name: startAP
nodes.summary: Starts the soft AP fallback for configuration when no home WiFi connection is available.

nodes.name: startMDNS
nodes.summary: Starts the mDNS responder advertising meganecan.local while in STA mode.

nodes.name: SaveCredentials
nodes.summary: Persists home WiFi SSID, password, and optional static IP to NVS.

nodes.name: BleMediaKeyboard.h
nodes.summary: Implements a BLE HID consumer-control keyboard the ESP advertises so the phone treats it as a media keyboard, sending play/pause/next/volume usage codes as an alternative to the AMS remote-command path.
nodes.languageNotes: Header-only class wrapping NimBLE HID device with a NimBLEServerCallbacks subclass to re-advertise on disconnect.

nodes.name: BleMediaKeyboard
nodes.summary: NimBLE HID keyboard peripheral that initializes the HID service, advertises a media-keyboard appearance, and writes consumer-control usage codes as notifications.

nodes.name: BusTap.h
nodes.summary: Declares the IBusTap observer interface with onTx/onRx hooks, allowing taps (serial mirror, WS recorder) to passively observe CAN frames flowing through the bus abstraction.

nodes.name: IBusTap
nodes.summary: Pure-virtual observer interface for CAN bus frame taps, exposing onTx and onRx callbacks invoked by the bus abstraction.

nodes.name: SerialMirrorTap.h
nodes.summary: IBusTap implementation that mirrors every outbound CAN frame to the serial @TX wire protocol so the PC-side virtual-display proxy can reassemble and render AFFA3 screens.

nodes.name: SerialMirrorTap
nodes.summary: Concrete bus tap that forwards transmitted CAN frames to WireProto::emitTx for serial mirroring.

nodes.name: SerialConsole.h
nodes.summary: Declares the SerialConsole namespace begin/loop entry points for the USB serial command interface processed in the main loop.

nodes.name: WireConsole.cpp
nodes.summary: Parses inbound wire-protocol command lines (@INJ frame injection, @EMU self-ack toggle, @KEY key events) from the PC proxy and dispatches them to the CAN ingest path and display.

nodes.name: handle
nodes.summary: Tokenizes a wire-protocol command line and routes @INJ, @EMU, and @KEY commands to frame injection (gotFrame), emu self-ack toggling, and display key processing.

nodes.name: WireConsole.h
nodes.summary: Declares the WireConsole command handler interface for processing wire-protocol input lines from the PC-side proxy.

nodes.name: main.cpp
nodes.summary: Firmware entry point: builds the display driver from NVS, wires the CAN bus, taps, BLE/AMS, WiFi, ELM, and HTTP server in setup(), and drives display ticks, media, time sync, and heap monitoring in loop().
nodes.languageNotes: Arduino setup()/loop() lifecycle; assembles global singletons and the AffaDisplayBase* driver chosen by display_type from NVS.

nodes.name: gotFrame
nodes.summary: General CAN receive callback: ingests inbound frames into HwCanBus, logs them via CanLog, and forwards them to the active display's recv().

nodes.name: restoreDisplay
nodes.summary: Restores persisted display state from NVS at boot, replaying last/welcome text with scroll effects depending on the saved autoRestore preference.

nodes.name: initDisplay
nodes.summary: Builds the concrete display driver (carminat / updatelist / updatelist_menu) from NVS config, wires bus, clock, skip_funcreg, ELM, and wire links, and selects the matching virtual display.

nodes.name: HandleKey
nodes.summary: Maps decoded AffaKey button events to either AMS remote commands or BLE HID media keyboard writes depending on the configured bt_mode.

nodes.name: setup
nodes.summary: Arduino setup: initializes serial console, display, AMS, Bluetooth, WiFi, HTTP server, CAN logging, wire links, bus taps, and the CAN controller with pins and callbacks.

nodes.name: loop
nodes.summary: Main loop: services serial/wire consoles, Bluetooth, media ticks, time sync, WiFi, heap watchdog, display tick/processEvents, and ELM updates.

nodes.name: WsRecorderTap
nodes.summary: IBusTap that mirrors received CAN frames to the WebSocket wire link via emitRxFrame for the browser-side recorder.

nodes.name: secrets.h
nodes.summary: Untracked header defining the soft-AP WiFi SSID and password macros used for the configuration access point.

nodes.name: CanLog.cpp
nodes.summary: Thread-safe ring buffer that records selected CAN frame IDs as text lines, with NVS-persisted enable/ID-filter config and JSON serialization for the web UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a FreeRTOS mutex (xSemaphore) to guard the shared log buffer accessed from CAN callback and HTTP threads.

nodes.name: onFrame
nodes.summary: Appends a formatted timestamped hex line for a CAN frame to the ring buffer when logging is enabled and the frame ID passes the filter set.

nodes.name: setConfig
nodes.summary: Updates the enable flag and ID-filter CSV under mutex, then persists the new logging configuration to NVS.

nodes.name: configJson
nodes.summary: Serializes the current CAN logging config (enabled flag and filter IDs CSV) to a JSON string for the web UI.

nodes.name: parseCsv
nodes.summary: Parses a comma-separated list of hex CAN IDs into the filter set used to gate which frames get logged.

nodes.name: dump
nodes.summary: Returns a snapshot copy of the buffered CAN log lines under mutex for display or transmission.

nodes.name: CanLog.h
nodes.summary: Declares the CanLog namespace API for CAN frame logging: begin, onFrame, dump, clear, setConfig, and configJson.

nodes.name: WireProto.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements the wire-protocol broker: registers WireLinks, builds @TX/@RX frame lines, fans emitted lines out to all links, and dispatches inbound command lines to a callback.

nodes.name: buildFrameLine
nodes.summary: Formats a CAN frame into a wire-protocol text line (tag, hex id, hex bytes) for transmission over serial or WebSocket links.

nodes.name: emitTx
nodes.summary: Builds a @TX frame line and emits it to every registered wire link, mirroring outbound CAN frames to the PC proxy.

nodes.name: WireProto.h
nodes.summary: Declares the WireProto broker namespace and the one UART/WS contract (@TX/@RX/@EV firmware->PC, @KEY/@INJ PC->firmware) used by serial and WebSocket wire links.

nodes.name: SerialWireLink.h
nodes.summary: WireLink implementation that emits wire-protocol lines over the USB serial port; always considered connected.

nodes.name: SerialWireLink
nodes.summary: Concrete WireLink that writes lines to Serial and reports a permanently connected transport.

nodes.name: WireLink.h
nodes.summary: Declares the abstract WireLink transport interface (emitLine, onCommand, connected) implemented by serial and WebSocket links for the wire protocol.

nodes.name: WireLink
nodes.summary: Pure-virtual transport interface for wire-protocol links, abstracting serial vs WebSocket emission and inbound command callbacks.

nodes.name: WsWireLink.cpp
nodes.summary: WebSocket WireLink implementation: attaches to a PsychicHttp WS endpoint, buffers emitted lines in a critical-section-guarded ring, and flushes batched output to all clients in loop().
nodes.languageNotes: Uses portENTER/EXIT_CRITICAL spinlocks to protect the TX buffer shared between CAN/HTTP contexts; flushes on size or time threshold.

nodes.name: attach
nodes.summary: Registers the WebSocket handler on the HTTP server, wiring open/close/frame callbacks and forwarding inbound frames to the command callback.

nodes.name: loop
nodes.summary: Flushes the buffered wire-protocol output to all WebSocket clients when the buffer is large enough or the flush interval has elapsed.

nodes.name: emitRxFrame
nodes.summary: Builds a @RX wire-protocol line for a received CAN frame and appends it to the WebSocket output buffer.

nodes.name: WsWireLink.h
nodes.summary: Declares the WsWireLink class, a WebSocket-backed WireLink with a fixed TX ring buffer, client tracking, and flush thresholds for streaming CAN/wire data to the browser.

nodes.name: WsWireLink
nodes.summary: WebSocket WireLink with a fixed buffer, mutex-guarded TX queue, and batched flushing to connected clients for the wire protocol.

nodes.name: ArduinoClock.h
nodes.summary: Concrete IClock implementation backed by Arduino's millis()/delay(), giving production code real wall time while tests substitute FakeClock.
nodes.languageNotes: Header-only adapter wrapping the Arduino time API behind the IClock interface.

nodes.name: HwCanBus.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements the real esp32_can/TWAI controller wrapper: forwards outbound frames through taps and the live-bus gate, and ingests inbound frames after marking RX activity.
nodes.languageNotes: busAlive gating (isLive) suppresses TX when no RX has been seen, preventing the TWAI controller from going bus-off on a bench board with no transceiver.

nodes.name: HwCanBus.h
nodes.summary: Declares HwCanBus, the production ICanBus implementation driving the ESP32 TWAI controller, with tap fan-out and a bus-alive RX window.

nodes.name: ICanBus.h
nodes.summary: Pure interface abstracting a CAN bus (send, onReceive, isLive, poll) so production hardware, loopback, and replay backends are interchangeable for testing.

nodes.name: IClock.h
nodes.summary: Pure time-source interface (millis, delayMs) enabling deterministic fake clocks in host-side unit tests versus real Arduino time on device.

nodes.name: LoopbackCanBus.h
nodes.summary: Header-only ICanBus test double that echoes sent frames into a fixed-capacity ring buffer, delivering them on poll() with drop accounting for overflow testing.
nodes.languageNotes: Fixed-size ring buffer (no heap) keeps the double allocation-free and counts dropped frames on overflow.

nodes.name: EmuBridge.cpp
nodes.summary: Wires a virtual display to the bus as a tap, looping firmware TX frames back into the virtual display and toggling self-ACK when emulation is enabled.

nodes.name: EmuBridge.h
nodes.summary: Declares EmuBridge, a bus tap that closes the loop between outbound firmware frames and a PC-side virtual display for bench emulation.

nodes.name: ReplayCanBus.cpp
nodes.summary: Parses captured @TX/@RX/@INJ wire-log text into timestamped CAN frames and replays them through onReceive, enabling golden-fixture tests against recorded traffic.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-rolled tokenizer (strtok_r/strtoul) parses the WireProto log grammar without dynamic allocation.

nodes.name: ReplayCanBus.h
nodes.summary: Declares ReplayCanBus, an ICanBus backend that loads recorded frames from text/file and steps them out on poll() for deterministic replay testing.

nodes.name: FakeClock.h
nodes.summary: Deterministic IClock test double whose time only advances when explicitly told (delayMs/advance), letting unit tests control timing precisely.

nodes.name: test_bus_foundation.cpp
nodes.summary: Unity test suite validating LoopbackCanBus echo, FIFO ordering, liveness, overflow drops, reentrant send, and FakeClock time control.

nodes.name: test_emubridge.cpp
nodes.summary: Unity test verifying EmuBridge closes the loop from firmware TX into a CarminatVirtualDisplay only when enabled, asserting the decoded screen contents.

nodes.name: test_radio_send.cpp
nodes.summary: Unity test driving an AffaDisplayBase subclass to verify ISO-TP framing over LoopbackCanBus and that sends are safely suppressed when the bus is not live.

nodes.name: test_replay.cpp
nodes.summary: Unity test exercising ReplayCanBus log-grammar parsing and golden-fixture playback into virtual displays, validating decoded now-playing and menu screens.

nodes.name: HwCanBus

nodes.name: send
nodes.summary: Notifies taps, gates transmission on isLive() to avoid bus-off, then forwards the frame to the underlying TWAI controller.

nodes.name: ingest
nodes.summary: Records RX activity for the bus-alive window and dispatches an inbound frame to the registered receive callback.

nodes.name: ICanBus
nodes.summary: Abstract CAN bus interface (send, onReceive, isLive, poll) implemented by hardware, loopback, and replay backends.

nodes.name: IClock
nodes.summary: Abstract time-source interface providing millis() and delayMs() for swappable real/fake clocks.

nodes.name: LoopbackCanBus
nodes.summary: Ring-buffered ICanBus test double that echoes sent frames back on poll(), tracking pending count and dropped overflow frames.

nodes.name: EmuBridge
nodes.summary: Bus tap that feeds firmware TX frames into a virtual display and routes the virtual display's replies back to the firmware receive path for closed-loop bench emulation.

nodes.summary: Captures the virtual display, receive callback, and clock references that the bridge uses when emulation is active.

nodes.name: ReplayCanBus
nodes.summary: ICanBus backend storing parsed recorded frames and stepping them out on poll()/step() for deterministic replay of captured CAN traffic.

nodes.name: AffaDisplayBase.cpp

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start here. The README explains why MeganeCAN exists: an ESP32 sits on the Renault Megane 2 CAN bus between the radio and the dashboard display, and can emulate the radio to render its own menus, bridge phone media over Bluetooth, expose a web UI, and run OBD diagnostics. CLAUDE.md is the companion engineering guide that drills into the wire protocol, the display class hierarchy, and the hard-won BLE/WiFi/CAN-safety invariants. Read both to get the mental model the rest of the tour fills in.

tour.title: Layered Architecture Map
tour.description: ARCHITECTURE.md lays out the firmware in swappable layers and names the test seams that make hardware-free testing possible, while FRAMEWORK.md describes the target EventBus/IModule lifecycle the codebase is migrating toward. Skim these to understand the design philosophy: pure interfaces (ICanBus, IClock, IDisplay) with interchangeable production vs. test implementations, kept lean because the device is RAM-tight (~62KB free).

tour.title: Firmware Entry Point
tour.description: main.cpp is the single highest-fan-out file (27 outbound edges) and the literal boot sequence. In setup() it builds the display driver from NVS config, wires the CAN bus and taps, starts BLE/AMS, WiFi, ELM, and the HTTP server; in loop() it drives display ticks, media updates, time sync, and a heap watchdog. Everything else in the tour is a subsystem that main.cpp assembles, so this file is your index into the whole project.

tour.title: Configuration & Wire Contract
tour.description: Building on the boot sequence, these utilities are the shared spine main.cpp depends on. AppConfig loads NVS settings (display_type, bt_mode, skip_funcreg, elm_enabled) into a RAM cache once at boot to avoid per-request NVS churn, and decides which display driver is built. WireProto defines the one UART/WebSocket contract (@TX/@RX/@EV firmware to PC, @KEY/@INJ PC to firmware) that the whole bench-testing story rides on, and Log is the timestamped serial logger used everywhere.

tour.title: CAN Bus Abstraction & Test Seams
tour.description: The CAN layer is built around pure interfaces so production and test backends are interchangeable. ICanBus abstracts send/receive/isLive; HwCanBus drives the real ESP32 TWAI controller and gates TX on received traffic (a bare bench board with no transceiver would otherwise reboot-loop into bus-off). LoopbackCanBus echoes frames for tests, and IClock (with FakeClock in tests vs ArduinoClock on device) makes timing deterministic. This interface-plus-fakes pattern is the key to the host test suite later in the tour.
tour.languageLesson: C++ pure-virtual interfaces (a class with only `= 0` methods) act like Java/TypeScript interfaces. Injecting an ICanBus*/IClock* pointer instead of hardcoding hardware is dependency injection — it lets the same logic run against real TWAI silicon on-device and against a loopback/fake-clock double on a PC, with no #ifdef build flags.

tour.title: AFFA3 Wire Protocol & Screen Model
tour.description: This is how raw CAN bytes become screens. IsoTp fragments an outbound payload into a first frame plus consecutive frames with protocol filler, and reassembles inbound multi-frame sequences. ScreenDecode turns a reassembled AFFA3 payload into a ScreenModel — the structured menu header, items, selection, and 8-segment text shared across all decoders. Frame.h is the transport-agnostic CAN frame struct (high fan-in) that flows through every layer. Together they are the protocol heart the README calls the AFFA3 wire protocol.
tour.languageLesson: ISO-TP (ISO 15765-2) is the standard transport layer for sending payloads larger than a single 8-byte CAN frame: one first frame announces the length, then numbered consecutive frames (here [0x20+N][6 bytes]) carry the rest, padded with a protocol-specific filler byte.

tour.title: Display Driver Hierarchy
tour.description: The display abstraction follows the README's chain: IDisplay (pure interface) to AffaDisplayBase (shared ISO-TP send plus the sync/registration state machine, high fan-in) down to the concrete CarminatDisplay (the large monochrome display, and the most complex driver). AffaCommonConstants defines the AffaKey button enum and the SyncStatus handshake bitmask used across drivers. initDisplay() in main.cpp picks one of these at boot from display_type. This is the polymorphism that lets one loop() drive several physically different OEM displays.

tour.title: UpdateList Display Variants
tour.description: The second radio protocol. UpdateListBase shares the AffaDisplayBase machinery but uses UpdateList CAN IDs and the 0x81 filler; UpdateListDisplay drives the text-only 8-segment display, while UpdateListMenuDisplay renders a full menu on the monochrome LCD. Contrasting these with the Carminat driver from Step 7 shows how the abstraction absorbs two protocols times two physical display types behind one IDisplay surface.

tour.title: Phone & Bluetooth Integration
tour.description: How song titles and notifications reach the display. The ESP advertises as a BLE peripheral; the iPhone connects, then the ESP takes a GATT client over that inbound link. bluetooth.cpp orchestrates advertising, bonding, and connection relaxation, then starts the AMS (now-playing plus remote commands), ANCS (notifications), and CTS (clock) clients. The AMS callback feeds setMediaInfo on the display from Steps 7-8. Note the invariant: BLE callbacks must never block or do a write-with-response, or they deadlock the host.

tour.title: Web Server, Config UI & Commands
tour.description: The control plane. HttpServerManager (second-highest fan-out) wraps PsychicHttp and registers every config, media, OTA, WiFi, diagnostics, and emulation route, plus the single unified /api/dashboard endpoint that the firmware serves to hold fewer keep-alive sockets (a deliberate RAM choice). DisplayCommands translates those HTTP requests into IDisplay calls, and WiFiManager owns STA-with-AP-fallback networking and mDNS. This is where a human configures and updates the device.

tour.title: OBD Diagnostics
tour.description: An optional subsystem that polls vehicle ECUs. MyELMManager opens a TCP connection to a WiFi ELM327 adapter and cycles a combined PID plan across ECU headers (7E0, 743, 744, 745, 74D) using a non-blocking tick() state machine, caching results as JSON. DiagPlanCommon defines the MetricDef model with letter-indexed byte-extraction helpers (U8, U16, getBIT), and each PidPlan_*.h declares the metrics for one ECU. The .ini PID table is the raw reverse-engineered source data behind these plans.

tour.title: The Virtual Display & Bench Seam
tour.description: The bench has no real display, so this layer fakes one. SerialMirrorTap copies every outbound CAN frame to the @TX wire protocol; EmuBridge loops those frames back into a VirtualDisplayBase (the common emulator engine doing ISO-TP reassembly plus registration auto-ACK), and CarminatVirtualDisplay decodes them via ScreenDecode from Step 6 into a renderable screen. serial_proxy.py is the PC-side program that reassembles @TX frames over USB and renders the AFFA3 screen in a browser — closing the loop without a car.

tour.title: Host Tests & Fixtures
tour.description: The payoff of all the interface seams. These Unity tests run on the host PC (the platformio native env) with no hardware: test_bus_foundation exercises the loopback bus and FakeClock, test_radio_send verifies ISO-TP framing and that TX is suppressed on a dead bus, test_emubridge confirms the firmware-TX-to-virtual-display loop, and test_vdisplay validates Carminat and UpdateList decode end to end. They lean on recorded .canlog fixtures for golden-frame replay.

tour.title: Build, Flash & CI
tour.description: The capstone: how it all ships. platformio.ini defines the three environments — esp32dev-mini (the ESP32-C3 car board), esp32dev (the bench WROOM), and native (the host tests from Step 13) — with library deps and the OTA partition and merge-bin hooks. partitions_ota.csv lays out dual OTA app slots plus NVS and LittleFS to support over-the-air updates. The CI workflow runs the native tests and builds both firmware targets on every push, so the seams stay honest.
tour.languageLesson: PlatformIO build environments ([env:...] sections) are like build matrices: each names a board, framework, and lib deps. A `native` env compiles the shared src/ logic with desktop GCC against the interface fakes, so unit tests run in CI seconds instead of requiring a flashed ESP32.
